topic: Sizing a solar panel and battery for an off-grid garden — the actual maths
voiceover_script: Sizing solar looks like black magic. It's actually three sums. Get it wrong and you're either paying for capacity you'll never use, or watching your battery die on the third cloudy day. First, your daily energy budget: a Raspberry Pi running all day, plus a pump and valves running for minutes, add up to about forty two watt-hours a day. Divide that by your sun hours and a seventy percent efficiency margin, and a fifty watt panel covers it with real headroom to spare. For the battery, size for three cloudy days in a row — that's a twenty amp-hour battery at twelve volts, lead-acid or lithium. That's it. Three sums, and your garden runs through a grey week without blinking. The full sizing calculator is in the parts list spreadsheet — link in bio.

scenes.text: Sizing solar looks like black magic. It's actually three sums.
Get it wrong and you're either paying for capacity you'll never use, or watching your battery die on the third cloudy day.
First, your daily energy budget: a Raspberry Pi running all day, plus a pump and valves running for minutes, add up to about forty two watt-hours a day.
Divide that by your sun hours and a seventy percent efficiency margin, and a fifty watt panel covers it with real headroom to spare.
For the battery, size for three cloudy days in a row — that's a twenty amp-hour battery at twelve volts, lead-acid or lithium.
That's it. Three sums, and your garden runs through a grey week without blinking.
The full sizing calculator is in the parts list spreadsheet — link in bio.
